title: Iran squad for FIFA World Cup 2026 — Mehdi Taremi included; Full list of players
original_text: Iran squad for FIFA World Cup 2026 — Mehdi Taremi included; Full list of players left File photo: In the group stage, Iran will face New Zealand on June 16 and Belgium on June 22 in Los Angeles, and ‌Egypt five days later in Seattle. REUTERS https://ss-i.thgim.com/public/incoming/dl5p6z/article71048893.ece/alternates/FREE_1200/2026-05-29T171552Z_1023317570_UP1EM5T1BYE5R_RTRMADP_3_SOCCER-FRIENDLY-IRN-GMB.JPG UMIT BEKTAS Soccer Football - International Friendly - Iran v Gambia - Mardan Sports Complex, Antalya, Turkey - May 29, 2026 Iran's Mehdi Taremi celebrates scoring their third goal with teammates REUTERS/Umit Bektas Iran, which has faced months of ​uncertainty over travel and security arrangements for the World Cup, will base its squad ⁠in the Mexican border city of Tijuana during the tournament. Cairo Iran coach Amir Ghalenoei named a 26-man squad for FIFA World Cup 2026 on Monday, ​relying on veterans Mehdi Taremi and Alireza Jahanbakhsh, while ‌leaving out Sardar Azmoun. Azmoun, who has scored ​57 goals in 91 internationals, was left ⁠out of the preliminary squad and did not make the final squad, despite Iran’s Vice President, Abdolkarim Hosseinzadeh, calling ‌for him to return. ALSO READ | South Africa football body says World Cup squad will depart Monday after visa delay https://sportstar.thehindu.com/football/south-africa-fifa-world-cup-2026-squad-sports-minister-gayton-mckenzie-administrative-error/article71047848.ece Azmoun, who plays for Shabab Al-Ahli in the United Arab Emirates, ‌was also omitted from the Iran squad ‌in March, with local media reporting he had ⁠been expelled from the national team for a perceived act of disloyalty to the government. IRAN SQUAD FOR FIFA WORLD CUP 2026 Goalkeepers: Alireza Beiranvand, Seyed ​Hossein Hosseini, Payam Niazmand Defenders: Danial Eiri, Ehsan Hajsafi, Saleh Hardani, Hossein Kanaani, Shoja Khalilzadeh, ⁠Milad Mohammadi, Ali Nemati, Ramin Rezaeian Midfielders: Rouzbeh Cheshmi, Saeid ⁠Ezatolahi, Mehdi Ghaedi, Saman Ghoddos, Mohammad Ghorbani, Alireza Jahanbakhsh, Mohammad Mohebi, Amir Mohammad Razzaghinia, ‌Mehdi Torabi, Aria Yousefi Forwards: Ali Alipour, Dennis Dargahi, Amirhossein Hosseinzadeh, Mehdi Taremi, Shahriar Moghanlou Taremi, 33, who plays for Olympiacos, and ​former Brighton & Hove ‌Albion winger Jahanbakhsh will lead the attack in the tournament, which will be held in the United States, Canada and Mexico from June 11 to ‌July 19. Iran, which has faced months of ​uncertainty over travel and security arrangements for the World Cup, will base its squad ⁠in the Mexican border city of Tijuana during the Finals, after the football world governing body, FIFA, approved ‌a request to move the training camp from Arizona. Iran is scheduled to play all three of its group matches in the United States. It will take on New Zealand on June 15 and Belgium on June 21 in Los Angeles, and ‌Egypt five days later in Seattle. Goalkeepers: Alireza Beiranvand, Seyed ​Hossein Hosseini, Payam Niazmand Defenders: Danial Eiri, Ehsan Hajsafi, Saleh Hardani, Hossein Kanaani, Shoja Khalilzadeh, ⁠Milad Mohammadi, Ali Nemati, Ramin Rezaeian Midfielders: Rouzbeh Cheshmi, Saeid ⁠Ezatolahi, Mehdi Ghaedi, Saman Ghoddos, Mohammad Ghorbani, Alireza Jahanbakhsh, Mohammad Mohebi, Amir Mohammad Razzaghinia, ‌Mehdi Torabi, Aria Yousefi Forwards: Ali Alipour, Dennis Dargahi, Amirhossein Hosseinzadeh, Mehdi Taremi, Shahriar Moghanlou
